mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 4.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2022-03-15 indicates potential for rapid tightening ahead
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with policy rates rising toward restrictive levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment of more than 100,000 persons signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100,000 persons in single month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,747 thousand as of 2022-03-15 represents near-peak employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness beginning to reverse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 100,000, or correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized rate signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,583.1 billion as of 2022-10-15 reflects recent economic momentum
regime_hint: post-expansion slowdown with growth decelerating toward stall-speed
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth < 1.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in industrial production index signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce cyclical equity allocations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.4016 as of 2022-02-15 shows recent production strength
regime_hint: supply-constrained environment with production beginning to contract
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration of retail sales (RSAFS decline > 1.0% month-over-month) and housing starts (HOUST decline > 10% month-over-month) signals synchronized demand collapse across consumer and residential sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.0% AND HOUST declines > 10% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 459,821 million and HOUST at 1,142 thousand as of 2022-02-15
regime_hint: demand shock regime with synchronized contraction across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or the interaction effect (joint decline) does not produce more negative returns than either series declining alone (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPIAUCSL combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) signals monetary policy in restrictive stance relative to inflation, which constrains equity valuations through higher real discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 240.305 and FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2022-02-15 and 2022-03-15 respectively
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with policy tightening lagging inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both CPIAUCSL inflation and FEDFUNDS are elevated do not differ significantly from returns when only one condition is met (interaction F-test p > 0.12), or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumer spending weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2022-03-15 remains near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion transitioning to contraction with labor market inflection point
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE increases > 0.5pp are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.12), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during labor market deterioration periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,000 thousand units annualized rate signals residential demand collapse that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as forward GDP growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,142 thousand as of 2022-02-15 shows housing activity above trigger threshold
regime_hint: demand-constrained regime with housing market cooling from elevated levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of instances when HOUST < 1,000 thousand, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST level and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
